# gh CLI Safe-Allowlist Command Matrix

**Source enumeration**: `gh-cli-commands.json` (gh 2.89.0, generated by `scripts/gh-allowlist-audit.sh`).
**Classification sources**: merged from `gh-classify-A.md` … `gh-classify-E.md` (5 parallel classifier agents, one per family cluster). Each manual-review case was cross-checked against `github.com/cli/cli` at `pkg/cmd/<family>/<verb>/` or resolved via DeepWiki on `cli/cli`.

## Totals

| Class           | Count | Notes |
|-----------------|-------|-------|
| SAFE            | 63    | Emits `"gh <family> <verb>": "allow"` + `"gh <family> <verb> *": "allow"`. |
| MUTATING        | 83    | Server-side POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE; no allow pattern, falls through to `*: ask`. |
| WRITES-LOCAL    | 9     | Writes files or gh config; no workspace scoping per plan §Open Questions. |
| AUTH            | 6     | Credential mutation; never auto-allow. |
| INTERACTIVE     | 10    | Blocks AI session (TTY / browser / long stream). |
| INSTALLS-CODE   | 6     | Downloads or runs arbitrary third-party code. 5 extension verbs demoted to explicit ask; `gh copilot` falls through to ask. |
| **Total leaves**| **183** | — |

SAFE verbs that accept side-effect flags (`--web`, `--edit`/`--editor`, `--output`, `--dir`, `--clobber`, `--watch`, `--follow`, `-w`) are re-asked by the **global gh flag demotion layer** in `opencode.jsonc`, emitted immediately after the per-family allows. `gh auth status --show-token` / `-t` is re-asked by its own per-command override because the flag leaks a credential even on a SAFE verb.

`gh extension install|remove|upgrade|exec|create` are **explicitly demoted to ask** at the absolute end of the bash block so the asks win over every prior rule — including `* --help` and `* --version` — preventing bypass via help/version suffixes. The user is prompted for each extension operation rather than blocked outright.

`gh api` is handled by the pre-existing three-layer GET-only block (broad allow → method/body-flag asks → explicit GET re-allow); it is left untouched.

## Rule evaluation semantics

`rules.findLast(wildcardMatch)`. `*` → `.*` under `s` flag; trailing ` *` becomes optional `( .*)?`. See `docs/design/opencode.md` § Rule Evaluation Semantics for full details.

## Per-family matrix

### auth (7)

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| gh auth login      | AUTH       | — | (no allow) |
| gh auth logout     | AUTH       | — | (no allow) |
| gh auth refresh    | AUTH       | — | (no allow) |
| gh auth setup-git  | AUTH       | — | (no allow) |
| gh auth status     | SAFE       | `--show-token`/`-t` (leaks credential) | `"gh auth status"`, `"gh auth status *"` + per-command demotions `"gh auth status --show-token*": "ask"`, `"gh auth status -t*": "ask"` |
| gh auth switch     | AUTH       | — | (no allow) |
| gh auth token      | AUTH       | — | (no allow, per pre-decided policy) |

| gh config list        | SAFE     | — | `"gh config list"`, `"gh config list *"`, `"gh config ls"`, `"gh config ls *"` |
| gh config set         | MUTATING | — | (no allow) |
| gh config clear-cache | MUTATING | — | (no allow) |

### ruleset (3)

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |

| gh ruleset list  | SAFE | `--web` | `"gh ruleset list"`, `"gh ruleset list *"` |
| gh ruleset view  | SAFE | `--web` | `"gh ruleset view"`, `"gh ruleset view *"` |

### alias (4)

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| gh alias list   | SAFE         | — | `"gh alias list"`, `"gh alias list *"` |
| gh alias set    | WRITES-LOCAL | — | (no allow; writes gh config; `--shell` makes alias run arbitrary commands) |
| gh alias delete | WRITES-LOCAL | — | (no allow; mutates gh config) |
| gh alias import | WRITES-LOCAL | — | (no allow; imports aliases into gh config) |

User-defined aliases (`gh <my-alias>`) expand to arbitrary commands and are deliberately NOT enumerated — they fall through to `*: ask`.

### extension (8)

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| gh extension list    | SAFE          | — | `"gh extension list"`, `"gh extension list *"` |
| gh extension search  | SAFE          | `--web` | `"gh extension search"`, `"gh extension search *"` |
| gh extension browse  | INTERACTIVE   | — | (no allow; TUI marketplace) |
| gh extension install | INSTALLS-CODE | — | **`"gh extension install*": "ask"`** |
| gh extension remove  | INSTALLS-CODE | — | **`"gh extension remove*": "ask"`** |
| gh extension upgrade | INSTALLS-CODE | — | **`"gh extension upgrade*": "ask"`** |
| gh extension exec    | INSTALLS-CODE | — | **`"gh extension exec*": "ask"`** |
| gh extension create  | INSTALLS-CODE | — | **`"gh extension create*": "ask"`** (scaffolds extension code on disk) |

Asks are placed at the **absolute end of the bash rules block** so they win over `* --help`, `* --version`, and any other prior rule. A user cannot bypass these asks by appending `--help`; each invocation prompts the user.

Note: gh 2.89.0 does NOT expose `gh extension view` — the enumeration shows only `browse`, `create`, `exec`, `install`, `list`, `remove`, `search`, `upgrade`. No pattern is emitted for a nonexistent verb.

| gh attestation trusted-root | SAFE         | `--verify-only` refreshes local TUF cache (accepted per `--cache` rubric row) | `"gh attestation trusted-root"`, `"gh attestation trusted-root *"` |
| gh attestation download     | WRITES-LOCAL | — | (no allow) |

| gh gpg-key add    | MUTATING | — | (no allow) |

|---|---|---|---|

| gh ssh-key add    | MUTATING | — | (no allow) |

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| gh org list | SAFE | — | `"gh org list"`, `"gh org list *"` |

### api (1)

| Command | Class | Side-effect flags | Proposed pattern(s) |
|---|---|---|---|
| gh api | SAFE-WITH-CAVEATS | — | (handled by the existing three-layer `gh api` block in `opencode.jsonc`: broad allow → method/body-flag asks → explicit GET re-allow) |

## Global gh flag demotion layer

Placed immediately after the per-family allows and before the `gh api` block in `opencode.jsonc`. Each of these patterns is last-match-wins over any SAFE allow when the corresponding flag is present:

```jsonc
"gh * --web*": "ask", "gh * -w *": "ask", "gh * -w": "ask",
"gh * --edit*": "ask",
"gh * --output*": "ask",
"gh * --dir *": "ask", "gh * --dir=*": "ask",
"gh * --clobber*": "ask",
"gh * --watch*": "ask",
"gh * --follow*": "ask",
```

These cover the side-effect flags flagged across all five classifier outputs: browser (`--web`/`-w`), editor (`--edit`/`--editor`), file writes (`--output`/`--dir`/`--clobber`), and long-running streams (`--watch`/`--follow`).

## Validation

- `scripts/gh-allowlist-simulator.py` ports `Wildcard.match` + `findLast` and runs 137 test cases covering every class, every global flag demotion, every explicit extension deny, and regression cases for the pre-existing `rm -rf` / `gh api` blocks. Must pass 100%.

## Re-run cadence

When `gh` is bumped in `Brewfile`, re-run `scripts/gh-allowlist-audit.sh` and diff this matrix. Newly-introduced leaves must be classified before they ship. CI wiring for this check is a follow-up (see plan §Open Questions #4).
